# 2026 FIFA World Cup — Group Stage Goals Per Match Statistics (Matchday 1 & 2)

## Tournament Overview
The 2026 FIFA World Cup, hosted across the United States, Canada, and Mexico, is the first edition with **48 teams** in **12 groups of 4**. The group stage consists of 3 matchdays per group, totaling **72 group stage matches**.

---

## Matchday 1 Results (June 11–17, 2026)

As of June 17, 2026, **20 of 24 Matchday 1 games have been completed** (Groups A–J). Groups K and L play their openers today.

| Group | Match | Score |
|-------|-------|-------|
| **A** | Mexico vs South Africa | **2–0** |
| **A** | South Korea vs Czech Republic | **2–1** |
| **B** | Canada vs Bosnia-Herzegovina | **1–1** |
| **B** | Switzerland vs Qatar | **1–1** |
| **C** | Brazil vs Morocco | **1–1** |
| **C** | Haiti vs Scotland | **0–1** |
| **D** | USA vs Paraguay | **4–1** |
| **D** | Australia vs Turkey | **2–0** |
| **E** | Germany vs Curaçao | **7–1** |
| **E** | Ivory Coast vs Ecuador | **1–0** |
| **F** | Netherlands vs Japan | **2–2** |
| **F** | Sweden vs Tunisia | **5–1** |

---

## Matchday 1 Goal-Scoring Statistics (20 completed matches)

- **Total goals scored**: **61 goals in 20 matches**
- **Average goals per match**: **3.05**
- **Highest-scoring match**: Germany 7–1 Curaçao (8 goals)
- **Lowest-scoring match**: Spain 0–0 Cape Verde (only goalless draw)
- **Draws**: 7 out of 20 matches (35%) — CAN-BIH, SUI-QAT, BRA-MAR, NED-JPN, BEL-EGY, IRN-NZL, KSA-URU
- **Clean sheets**: 5 (Mexico, Scotland, Australia, Ivory Coast, Argentina)

### Notable Individual Performances:
- **Lionel Messi**: Hat-trick vs Algeria — tied Miroslav Klose's all-time record of 16 career World Cup goals
- **Kai Havertz**: Brace vs Curaçao
- **Kylian Mbappé**: Brace vs Senegal
- **Erling Haaland**: 2 goals on his World Cup debut vs Iraq
- **Folarin Balogun**: Brace vs Paraguay (first US brace since 1930)
- **Yasin Ayari**: Brace vs Tunisia
- **Elijah Just**: Brace for New Zealand vs Iran

---

## Matchday 2 Status

**Matchday 2 has NOT yet begun.** It is scheduled for June 18–23, 2026:

| Groups | Date |
|--------|------|
| A & B | June 18 |
| C & D | June 19 |
| E & F | June 20 |
| G & H | June 21 |
| I & J | June 22 |
| K & L | June 23 |

---

## Historical Comparison: Goals Per Match at FIFA World Cups

| Tournament | Goals/Match |
|-----------|-------------|
| 1954 (all-time high) | **5.38** |
| 2014 | **2.67** |
| 2018 | **2.64** |
| 2022 | **2.69** |
| 1990 (all-time low) | **2.21** |
| **2026 (MD1, 20 games)** | **~3.05** |

The 2026 tournament's early rate of ~3.05 goals/match is **significantly above** the recent World Cup average (~2.65), boosted by blowouts like Germany–Curaçao (7–1) and Sweden–Tunisia (5–1). However, this rate may normalize as the tournament progresses.

---

## Key Storylines
- **3 red cards** in the opening match (Mexico vs South Africa) — a World Cup opening match record
- **Cape Verde** earned their first-ever World Cup point on debut (0–0 vs Spain)
- **Qatar** earned their first-ever World Cup point (1–1 vs Switzerland via a late own goal)
- **Scotland** won their first World Cup match since 1990 (1–0 vs Haiti)
- **Curaçao** scored their first-ever World Cup goal (Comenencia) despite losing 7–1 to Germany
- The expanded 48-team format has produced competitive matches, with 35% ending as draws

key_facts: As of June 17, 2026, 20 of 24 Matchday 1 group stage games have been completed (Groups A–J); Groups K and L play today.
Matchday 2 has NOT yet begun; it is scheduled June 18–23, 2026.
61 goals have been scored in the 20 completed Matchday 1 matches, for an average of approximately 3.05 goals per match.
Germany 7–1 Curaçao is the highest-scoring match so far (8 total goals).
Spain 0–0 Cape Verde is the only goalless draw through Matchday 1.
Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick vs Algeria (3–0), tying Miroslav Klose's all-time record of 16 career World Cup goals.
7 out of 20 completed Matchday 1 games ended as draws (35%).
The 2026 goals-per-match rate (~3.05) is significantly above the 2022 World Cup average (~2.69) and recent tournament norms (~2.65).
3 red cards were shown in the opening match (Mexico vs South Africa), a World Cup opening match record.
The expanded 48-team format (12 groups of 4) means 72 total group stage matches across 3 matchdays.
Cape Verde, Curaçao, and Qatar all achieved historic firsts: Cape Verde earned their first WC point, Curaçao scored their first WC goal, Qatar earned their first WC point.
Notable braces on Matchday 1: Havertz (GER), Mbappé (FRA), Haaland (NOR), Balogun (USA), Ayari (SWE), Just (NZL).
